title: sideritis syriaca subsp. nusairiensis
intro: Sideritis syriaca subsp. nusairiensis is a rare subspecies of the Sideritis syriaca plant, commonly known as mountain tea, which belongs to the Lamiaceae family. It is native to the Mediterranean region and has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in the treatment of respiratory issues such as bronchitis and coughs. The plant contains flavonoids and essential oils with anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which contribute to its health benefits, including reducing inflammation, improving digestion, and boosting the immune system. Sideritis syriaca subsp. nusairiensis can be prepared as a tea by infusing its dried leaves in hot water, or it can be used in herbal infusions, capsules, or tinctures to harness its medicinal properties.
